title: Ulysses and the Sirens
caption: Waterhouse depicts Odysseus straining against his bonds as the Sirens beckon from their island.

title: The Barque of Dante
caption: Delacroix's debut masterpiece plunges Dante and Virgil into the roiling waters of the Styx.

title: The School of Athens
caption: Raphael gathers every great philosopher of antiquity under a single vaulted heaven.

title: Snow Storm — Steam-Boat off a Harbour's Mouth
Ophelia
caption: Millais' Ophelia floats downstream in a Pre-Raphaelite vision of Shakespeare's drowned heroine.

title: Liberty Leading the People
caption: Delacroix's allegory of the 1830 Revolution personifies Liberty as a woman leading Parisians over the barricades.

title: The Nightmare
caption: Fuseli's incubus crouching on a sleeping woman became the defining image of Romantic horror.

title: Wanderer Above the Sea of Fog
caption: Friedrich's wanderer surveys an infinite landscape, the quintessential Romantic icon of individual freedom.

title: The Death of Socrates
caption: David dramatizes the philosopher's calm acceptance of death while his students weep around him.

title: The Oxbow
caption: Cole's split composition contrasts untamed wilderness with the cultivated Connecticut River valley.

title: Ivan the Terrible and His Son
Heart of the Andes
caption: Church's monumental vision of South American landscape drew crowds who viewed it through opera glasses.

title: The Sea of Ice
caption: Friedrich's shattered ice sheets crushing a ship embody the Romantic confrontation with nature's indifference.

title: The Great Wave off Kanagawa
caption: Hokusai's iconic wave dwarfs Mount Fuji, capturing the terrifying beauty of nature's indifference.

title: Abbey in the Oakwood
caption: Friedrich's desolate abbey ruin and bare oaks create a meditation on mortality and the passage of time.

title: The Slave Ship
caption: Turner's seascape transforms a slave trader's crime into a cosmic reckoning of fire and water.

title: Pandemonium
caption: Martin's vast vision of Satan's capital city in Hell dwarfs its demonic inhabitants with impossible architecture.

title: Destruction (The Course of Empire)
caption: Cole's fourth painting in The Course of Empire shows civilization at the moment of its violent collapse.

title: Napoleon Crossing the Alps
caption: David's propaganda portrait shows Bonaparte mastering nature itself as he crosses the Great St Bernard Pass.

title: Macbeth
caption: Martin's theatrical vision of the Scottish play captures the supernatural atmosphere of Shakespeare's tragedy.

title: Ulysses Deriding Polyphemus
caption: Turner dissolves Homer's Cyclops episode into a blaze of Mediterranean light as Odysseus taunts the blinded giant.

title: Oath of the Horatii
caption: David's manifesto of Neoclassical painting depicts three brothers swearing to fight or die for Rome.

title: The Birth of Venus
caption: Botticelli's iconic Venus arrives on the shore of Cyprus, a Renaissance fusion of classical myth and Christian grace.

title: Primavera
caption: Botticelli's garden of perpetual spring weaves Ovidian mythology into an allegory of love and renewal.

title: Hylas and the Nymphs
caption: Waterhouse depicts the fatal moment when water nymphs lure Hercules' beloved companion into a moonlit pool.

title: Echo and Narcissus
caption: Waterhouse captures the tragedy of Echo pining for Narcissus as he gazes entranced at his own reflection.

title: The Creation of Adam
caption: Michelangelo's near-touching fingers of God and Adam became the supreme image of divine creativity and human potential.

title: The Last Supper
caption: Leonardo freezes the moment of Christ's announcement of betrayal, each apostle reacting with individual psychological truth.

title: The Return of the Prodigal Son
caption: Rembrandt's final masterpiece distills the parable into an embrace of unconditional mercy, glowing from the darkness.

title: Sacrifice of Isaac
caption: Caravaggio freezes Abraham's blade mid-descent as the angel intervenes to spare Isaac in violent chiaroscuro.

title: The Tower of Babel
caption: Bruegel's spiraling tower captures humanity's overreaching ambition and the chaos of mutual incomprehension.

title: The Garden of Earthly Delights
caption: Bosch's hallucinatory triptych maps humanity's journey from Edenic innocence through earthly excess to infernal punishment.

title: Dante and Virgil in Hell
caption: Bouguereau depicts two damned souls locked in savage combat as Dante and Virgil watch in the eighth circle of Hell.

title: Paolo and Francesca da Rimini
caption: Rossetti follows the doomed lovers from their fatal kiss to their eternal punishment in Dante's whirlwind.

title: The Lady of Shalott
caption: Waterhouse depicts Tennyson's cursed Lady setting forth on her final voyage toward Camelot and death.

title: The Monk by the Sea
caption: Friedrich reduces landscape to elemental bands of earth, sea and sky, dwarfing a solitary figure before the void.

title: The Fighting Temeraire
caption: Turner's beloved painting shows the old warship towed to the breakers beneath a blazing sunset, an elegy for the age of sail.

title: Rain, Steam, and Speed
caption: Turner dissolves a Great Western Railway locomotive into atmosphere, celebrating and lamenting the industrial age.

title: The Raft of the Medusa
caption: Géricault's monumental canvas of shipwreck survivors scandalized France and launched Romantic painting.

title: Girl with a Pearl Earring
caption: Vermeer's enigmatic tronie captures a girl's glance over her shoulder, luminous against a dark void.

title: The Milkmaid
caption: Vermeer elevates a humble kitchen scene into a monument of quiet dignity through his mastery of light.

title: The Night Watch
caption: Rembrandt transforms a civic militia portrait into a theatrical drama of light, shadow and collective action.

title: The Anatomy Lesson of Dr. Nicolaes Tulp
caption: Rembrandt's early masterpiece unites the drama of Baroque painting with the Enlightenment pursuit of anatomical knowledge.

title: The Astronomer
caption: Vermeer bathes a scholar reaching toward a celestial globe in golden window light, celebrating quiet intellectual pursuit.

title: Philosopher in Meditation
caption: Rembrandt's spiraling staircase and dramatic light create an allegory of the contemplative life.

title: Among the Sierra Nevada, California
caption: Bierstadt's luminous panorama presented the American West as an earthly paradise.

title: The Ninth Wave
caption: Aivazovsky's survivors cling to wreckage as a titanic wave rises before a golden dawn, balancing terror and hope.

title: Bogatyrs
caption: Vasnetsov's three legendary warriors survey the Russian steppe, embodying the nation's mythic self-image.

title: Portrait of Dostoevsky
caption: Perov's definitive portrait captures the novelist's inward gaze and tortured spiritual intensity.

title: Isle of the Dead
caption: Böcklin's mysterious island sanctuary, approached by a shrouded figure, became the supreme Symbolist image of death.

title: The Ambassadors
caption: Holbein's double portrait hides an anamorphic skull beneath displays of worldly learning, a memento mori in disguise.

title: The Arnolfini Portrait
caption: Van Eyck's enigmatic double portrait revolutionized oil painting with its luminous surfaces and intricate symbolic program.

title: Bacchus and Ariadne
caption: Titian captures the god of wine leaping from his chariot toward the abandoned Ariadne in a riot of colour and movement.

title: The Return of Persephone
caption: Leighton's luminous canvas shows Hermes returning Persephone from the underworld to her waiting mother Demeter.

title: The Intervention of the Sabine Women
The Death of Marat
institution: Royal Museums of Fine Arts, Brussels

title: Gallery of Views of Ancient Rome
Phidias Showing the Frieze of the Parthenon
The Assumption of the Virgin
institution: Basilica di Santa Maria Gloriosa dei Frari

title: Landscape with the Fall of Icarus
institution: Royal Museums of Fine Arts, Brussels

title: Aristotle with a Bust of Homer
Seaport with the Embarkation of the Queen of Sheba
Snow Storm: Hannibal and His Army Crossing the Alps
The Sleep of Reason Produces Monsters
The Great Day of His Wrath
Sadak in Search of the Waters of Oblivion
Circe Offering the Cup to Ulysses
A Storm in the Rocky Mountains, Mt. Rosalie
Arrangement in Grey and Black No.1
Where Do We Come From? What Are We? Where Are We Going?
The Burial of the Count of Orgaz
The Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse
The Adoration of the Mystic Lamb (Ghent Altarpiece)
The Burning of the Houses of Lords and Commons
Hamlet and Horatio in the Graveyard
The Great Red Dragon and the Woman Clothed in Sun
King Cophetua and the Beggar Maid
Still Life with Lemons, Oranges and a Rose
Design for the Grande Galerie of the Louvre
The Morning of the Battle of Agincourt, 25th October 1415
caption: Gilbert paints the pre-dawn hours before Agincourt — Henry's rain-soaked army in the mud on St. Crispin's morning, atmosphere weighted between glory and catastrophe.
institution: Guildhall Art Gallery, City of London

title: Portrait of King Richard III
caption: The earliest surviving portrait of Richard III — a posthumous Tudor-era copy of a lost original. The face Shakespeare inherited, filtered through the same dynasty whose propaganda the play works inside.

title: Lord Byron in Albanian Dress
caption: Phillips's iconic portrait of the young Byron in the Albanian costume he bought in Epirus — the image that fixed Byron as a Romantic type. For Don Juan the painting is more than period decoration: the poem is about Byron-the-narrator more than about Juan-the-character, and this is the face of that narrator.

title: Orlando's Madness
caption: Doré's plate for the 1877 edition depicts the poem's titular moment — Orlando, on discovering the forest carvings that prove Angelica's love for Medoro, stripped of his armor and uprooting trees in his madness. The 1877 Hachette edition's engravings are the canonical Western visual tradition for Ariosto, the same project that produced Doré's Dante and Milton work.

title: Elizabeth I (The Armada Portrait)
artist: formerly attributed to George Gower; workshop
caption: Painted to celebrate the defeat of the Spanish Armada in August 1588, the portrait shows Elizabeth I in jewel-heavy imperial pomp, her hand resting on a globe at the Americas, the Spanish fleet wrecking on the storms visible through the windows behind her. Spenser's Gloriana is this queen under allegorical veil; the cover acknowledges that The Faerie Queene is inextricable from the sovereign it addresses.
institution: Three contemporary versions survive: Woburn Abbey (Bedford), the National Museum of the Royal Navy (Greenwich, bequeathed by the Tyrwhitt-Drake family), and the National Portrait Gallery (London). The Greenwich version is the one most often reproduced; source here is a composite public-domain scan.
